mnemonics: Distributed Systems are like a big team working together, D-C-A!
keyPoints: 15.1: Distributed Mutual Exclusion - A way to coordinate access to shared resources among processes in a distributed system.
15.2: Distributed Mutual Exclusion - A deadlock occurs when two or more processes are blocked, waiting for each other to release resources.
15.3: Distributed Mutual Exclusion - A necessary condition for deadlock is that the processes involved must be mutually exclusive - only one process can access a resource at a time.
15.4: Distributed Mutual Exclusion - Algorithms for avoiding deadlocks include Banker's Algorithm and Peterson's Algorithm.
15.5: Distributed Mutual Exclusion - An important concept to remember is that deadlock can be avoided by eliminating the possibility of infinite loops of resource requests and releases.
